items.title: Residents fear for liveability as government confirms reduced block sizes

children.content: The Northern Territory government is planning to reduce the minimum block size in low-density suburbs from 800 to 450 square metres, sparking concern from some residents.

image.altText: A housing subdivision with half of the section built

items.title: Outback tour company sues vehicle manufacturer over fatal NT rollover

children.content: An Alice Springs tour company is suing a vehicle manufacturer for negligence and breach of contract following a bus crash that killed a 69-year-old man in 2022.

image.altText: Patrick Bedford from Tourism Central Australia says JobKeeper needs to be extended

items.title: Aircraft from 15 countries touch down in Darwin ahead of war games

children.content: Military aircraft and aviators from 20 countries land in Darwin ahead of Exercise Pitch Black 2026, with the biennial war games poised to begin.

gallery.altText: The silhouette of a transport plane outline in the sunrise.
An aviator sits on the edge of a fighter jet's open cockpit.
Three air force officers stand in front of other personnel with fighter jets in the background.

thumbnail.altText: The silhouette of a transport plane outline in the sunrise.
An aviator sits on the edge of a fighter jet's open cockpit.

items.title: NT Police officer fires gun twice while 'trying to stop stolen vehicle'

children.content: A Northern Territory police officer has fired two shots at a vehicle while responding to a reported armed break-in in Palmerston.

image.altText: Close up of Queensland Police Service-issued black gun in holster on navy pants

items.title: Early-onset bowel cancer becoming more prevalent as signs are missed

children.content: Georgie Cauchi had blood in her stool, constant pain and was struggling to get out of bed, but still in her 20s doctors told her she was too young to have bowel cancer. When it was finally discovered several years later, the tumour had grown so large it was blocking her entire bowel.

image.altText: A young woman in a white shirt sitting in her living room.

items.title: Hundreds gather in Alice Springs ahead of Pine Gap protest

children.content: Hundreds of activists and academics have gathered in Alice Springs to join Arrernte traditional owners ahead of a planned protest against the joint Australian-US military base at Pine Gap.

gallery.altText: Two older women smile for the camera.

thumbnail.altText: Two older women smile for the camera.

items.title: 'Bring back public flogging': Inquiry hears of vicious online trolling

children.content: Racial slurs, online trolls and fears of far-right violence have taken a toll on First Nations people, a federal inquiry heard this week.

image.altText: Racism Hearing Inquiry committee

items.title: Fugitive busted pretending to fish as driver stuck in outback 'nightmare'

children.content: After agreeing to help smuggle an alleged drug trafficker out of Australia, Stephen Roche found himself at the centre of a living "nightmare", stranded in the outback without funds or friends, court documents have revealed.

image.altText: A man in a blue suit and green tie leaves court.

items.title: For some in Australia, hope is fading and anger is rising

children.content: Sulaiman Zandinan says Australia is amazing. Wendy Newman says people in the bush are "not country bumpkins". The ABC is inviting you to have Your Say about the issues, challenges and opportunities that shape your life in 2026.

cta.title: What do you want to tell us? Have Your Say

gallery.altText: A man with curly hair smiles while holding a sports whistle looking away

thumbnail.altText: A man with curly hair smiles while holding a sports whistle looking away

items.title: 'Open door to fraud': European body slams FIFA's World Cup integrity

children.content: Europe's top human rights body hits out at FIFA over the integrity of the World Cup and calls for change before the 2030 tournament.

image.altText: A group of people watch the football from a box.

items.title: US ally Jordan summons Iranian ambassador after missile strikes

children.content: The US warns its citizens to stay away from air and sea ports at Aqaba in southern Jordan, hours before Israeli authorities announced that Iranian missiles had been launched towards the city.

image.altText: A satellite imagery of an air base with helicopters and planes.

items.title: Lesbian couple told they are no longer the same family by fertility clinic

children.content: The Queensland Health advice, given to fertility providers to help navigate the state's assisted reproductive technology legislation, has had a "devastating" impact on some same-sex couples trying to have more children.

items.title: Alone, robbed, and in pain: A man finds himself on Sydney's streets

children.content: People with lived experience of homelessness say the issue is deepening, as housing stock remains out-of-reach and community providers are left struggling to cope with demand.

image.altText: Group of women outdoors around table

items.title: Home Affairs asks DV survivors to prove abuser relationship 'genuine'

children.content: A federal government safety net has been identified as one of the biggest stressors for migrant women experiencing domestic and family violence.

image.altText: An anonymous woman in the city, back of head

items.title: Asylum seekers sent Australia to Nauru say they are going hungry

children.content: According to a survey of 78 asylum seekers on Nauru, 99 per cent are regularly skipping meals because they cannot not afford to buy enough food, while more than three-quarters rarely have access to free, clean drinking water.

gallery.altText: A coastline with ship-loading equipment. The ocean is in the foreground and there are trees on the land.
Refugees and asylum seekers wander around the detention centre on Nauru

items.title: Holocaust museum staff and volunteers targeted by hateful comments

children.content: Melbourne Holocaust Museum has had to step up security since 2020 following a rise in incidents that have seen threatening voice messages and hateful comments left online.

image.altText: Breann Fellon standing next to a stain glass window

items.title: Rules to tighten on AI use in decision-making by government departments

children.content: Agencies like Centrelink and Services Australia will have to follow a framework to ensure decision-making is "fair and accurate" if they use AI.

image.altText: Prime minister Anthony Albanese

items.title: Teacher aides face rise in kicking, biting from students

children.content: About four hundred staff — the majority teacher aides — have complained to the United Workers Union since April about facing violent behaviour from students and parents.

image.altText: Unidentified student and teacher outside a classroom with a bag rack on display.

items.title: How to switch off and improve work-life balance when your job is online

children.content: When being across trends in your industry feels like part of your job, it can feel like work never ends. Here's how to protect your free time.

items.title: Try this week's history quiz (we made it an easy one)

children.content: Consider yourself a history buff? Test your smarts on the first Moon landing, John Howard and the Riot Act.

image.altText: Australian prime minister-elect John Howard during his first press conference

children.content: From Mexican wrestling to Indian slums, catch up on every week seven film from Race Around the World.

image.altText: Lucinda stands in front of the Eiffel Tower

items.title: Russia pounds Kyiv in major ballistic missile attack

children.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Russia battered Ukraine's capital Kyiv overnight into Sunday local time, with one of its biggest ballistic missile barrages of the war.

items.title: Road plan for new airport 'flawed and shortsighted', critics say

children.content: Some transport planners say the design of a major Western Sydney road upgrade is "nonsensical" and is at odds with the government's commitment to public transport and new housing.

image.altText: An ariel view of traffic on Fifteenth Avenue in Liverpool

items.title: Minister insists Tasmania won't lose health services under sale to Catholic provider

children.content: Doctors are concerned Tasmanians will be forced to travel interstate for certain procedures if the Hobart Private Hospital is sold to Catholic provider Calvary, but the health minister insists there will be no service gap.

image.altText: Exterior signage at Hobart Private Hospital.

items.title: Rising pedestrian deaths in Victoria prompt calls to improve safety

children.content: Pedestrian group Victoria Walks wants $150 million spent over four years to install or upgrade crossings, so people can safely cross the road.

image.altText: A woman with long red hair stands next to a pedestrian crossing button.

items.title: Paramedics told not to take complex cardiac patients to Mersey hospital

children.content: Paramedics have been told to transport complex cardiac patients in Tasmania's north-west to the Launceston General Hospital rather than the local Mersey Community Hospital.

image.altText: A hospital exterior

items.title: Actor Terence Donovan remembered by son Jason as 'larger than life'

children.content: Jason Donovan has paid tribute to his father who has died at the age of 90 after decades on Australian screens.

items.title: The 'terrifying' lab test results from store-bought bottle of vodka

children.content: Australia's bootleg booze market is booming and an alarming new discovery from a bottle of vodka sold in Melbourne reveals it is turning into a major health problem.

items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested on sex trafficking offences

children.content: British prosecutors say they are seeking the extradition of the Tate brothers from the US on rape and sex trafficking charges.

items.title: Antonelli extends F1 championship lead, heartbroken Russell crashes out in Belgium

children.content: Kimi Antonelli has extended his lead at the top of the F1 championship standings with victory in the Belgian Grand Prix, with Oscar Piastri overcoming an early knock and George Russell's race ending in heartbreak.

items.title: Hinkley set to coach Devils, Caddy taken to hospital as Dons end drought

children.content: Tasmania prepares to unveil Ken Hinkley as its inaugural AFL coach as Essendon snaps a 12-game losing streak despite losing Nate Caddy early.

items.title: Tom Trbojevic gutted after another injury, Titans, Dolphins record wins

items.title: Bek never thought she would grow old until she left her church behind

items.title: Going beyond the archives to revive a street's lost history

children.content: It's been a three year labour of love probing into the past and building on the fragments of a hidden history to write 'Logan St' and get it to the stage.
